dimension: Diagram coverage — DIAGRAM_PLAN.md (8 diagrams) vs final architecture needs
summary: Mostly covered for the structural/topology and proof-spine flows, but three load-bearing diagrams are missing (AgentJob lifecycle state machine, cross-store deletion saga, worker supervision/sleep-wake lifecycle), several DOMAIN_MODEL state machines are only rendered as sequence diagrams (losing recovery/branch states), and every "Spec anchors" reference points at draft section numbers that must be remapped to final ARCHITECTURE.md anchors with no placement guidance.

findings.finding: No diagram for the AgentJob lifecycle state machine. DOMAIN_MODEL defines a 10-state machine with admission control and budget/retry/terminal branches (created -> admitted -> provider_selected -> running -> schema_validated -> accepted | rejected | cancelled_budget | failed_retryable | failed_terminal), and ING-7 job-admission tool-stripping is a security-critical transition. The 8 planned diagrams only show AgentJob as a single node inside the meeting sequence (#1) and provider routing (#3) flows; its lifecycle (especially admission rejection and budget cancellation) is never drawn.
findings.evidence: docs/planning/DOMAIN_MODEL.md §State Machines 'Agent Job' (lines 61-63); DIAGRAM_PLAN.md sub-diagrams #1 and #3 (lines 28-79) reference AgentJob only as a flow node; PRD §ING-7 / WS admission referenced in prd line 13
findings.proposed_fix: Add a Mermaid stateDiagram-v2 for AgentJob lifecycle (admission/tool-stripping, provider selection, schema-validate, budget-cancel, retryable vs terminal failure), anchored to the final ARCHITECTURE.md Provider/Runtime Broker section (draft §7).

findings.finding: No diagram for the cross-store deletion saga. RET-2/REQ-F-013 is a P0 multi-store compensating purge (Markdown via KnowledgeWriter, GBrain index/graph, control-plane event store, audit tombstone, external links) with explicit failure/compensation states (partial purge, GBrain purge failure, dangling external refs) and a hard acceptance proof ('re-indexing from Markdown does not resurrect it'). For a production-grade build this distributed compensating operation is exactly what a diagram should make concrete, yet none of the 8 diagrams covers it.
findings.evidence: docs/planning/USER_FLOWS.md Flow 7 (lines 109-123); docs/planning/REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-F-013 (line 21); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §9 'Retention/deletion' (line 272); PRD §9.10 RET-2 (line 567) and acceptance 'Cross-store deletion' (lines 1074, 1114); DIAGRAM_PLAN.md has no matching diagram
findings.proposed_fix: Add a Mermaid sequenceDiagram or stateDiagram-v2 for the deletion saga showing per-store removal order, audit tombstone, and compensating-failure handling, anchored to the Temporal Workflows / Knowledge sections (draft §9/§6).

findings.finding: No diagram for worker supervision / sleep-wake-restart lifecycle. PRD §9.8 LIFE-1..7 (P0) plus §13 deployment require: Electron main supervising the control-plane worker, single-active-instance lease, durable-schedule catch-up (run-once-collapsed), in-flight Temporal resume with exactly-once envelope reuse, ordered on-wake replay (KnowledgeWriter writes before GBrain index jobs), and DB-unavailable degraded mode. This is the single biggest local-first risk per the PRD, but no diagram depicts the worker/process lifecycle, supervision, or wake catch-up sequence.
findings.evidence: PRD §9.8 LIFE-1..6 (lines 545-550), on-wake ordering (line 876), §20.2 sleep/wake proofs (lines 1108, 1132); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §13 (lines 335-344) and §4 degraded mode (lines 156-159); DOMAIN_MODEL invariant on separate persistence (line 81); DIAGRAM_PLAN.md has no lifecycle/supervision diagram
findings.proposed_fix: Add a Mermaid stateDiagram-v2 (or sequenceDiagram) for worker supervision/lifecycle: launch + lease acquisition, normal run, sleep/quit, wake -> catch-up evaluation -> ordered replay -> in-flight resume, and DB-unavailable degraded mode. Anchor to the Deployment/Lifecycle section (draft §13).

findings.finding: Every 'Spec anchors' line in DIAGRAM_PLAN.md references ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md section numbers (§2, §2.5, §3..§11, §17, Appendix A) — including the Full-Scope diagram literally citing 'ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §2'. When /arch-finalize emits the binding ARCHITECTURE.md from the template, sections will be renumbered/restructured, so these anchors will dangle. The plan also gives only 'Spec anchors' (what each diagram relates to), never a placement anchor (which final § embeds each diagram), so there is no instruction on where each of the 8 diagrams should live.
findings.evidence: DIAGRAM_PLAN.md lines 22, 43, 60, 77, 94, 111, 127, 142, 160 (all cite draft § numbers; line 22 names the draft file directly)
findings.proposed_fix: During finalization, remap each diagram's 'Spec anchors' to the final ARCHITECTURE.md section IDs and add an explicit 'Placement: §X' field per diagram so /tasks-gen and reviewers know where each diagram is embedded.
findings.anchor: Spec Anchor Index (draft §18) and each diagram-bearing section

findings.finding: Three DOMAIN_MODEL state machines are only (at best) rendered as sequence/flow diagrams, which lose their failure/recovery branch states. Meeting Closeout (#1 sequenceDiagram) omits recovery states needs_routing_review/provider_failed/schema_rejected/write_conflict/approval_pending/outbox_retry/completed_with_warnings; Knowledge Mutation (#4 sequenceDiagram) omits sync_lagging/parity_defect; the Approval and Source state machines have no dedicated state diagram at all (Approval is only implied inside #5 External Write Envelope).
findings.evidence: docs/planning/DOMAIN_MODEL.md §State Machines (lines 46-67: Meeting Closeout, Knowledge Mutation, Source, Approval); DIAGRAM_PLAN.md #1 (sequenceDiagram, line 44), #4 (sequenceDiagram, line 95), #5 (line 112)
findings.proposed_fix: Either add small stateDiagram-v2 companions for Meeting Closeout (with recovery states), Knowledge Mutation (parity/sync-lag), Approval, and Source ingestion, or explicitly note in DIAGRAM_PLAN that these state machines are documented as tables only — don't leave them implicitly 'covered' by a happy-path sequence diagram.
findings.anchor: Knowledge and GBrain (draft §6); Provider/Runtime (draft §7); Connector/Tool Gateways (draft §8)

findings.finding: Diagram #7 'Parallel Build Track DAG' risks duplicating the §2.5 'Subsystem Dependency DAG' already embedded in the draft (both flowchart TD over the same §3..§12 subsystems). Without a clear distinction the final doc may carry two near-identical DAGs that drift.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §2.5 dependency DAG already present (lines 78-103); DIAGRAM_PLAN.md #7 Parallel Build Track DAG (lines 131-144, spec anchors §2.5/§17/Appendix A)
findings.proposed_fix: Clarify that #7 is a team/build-track sequencing DAG (contract-first, integration order) distinct from §2.5's import-direction dependency DAG, or merge them with an overlay; ensure only one canonical dependency DAG is the source of truth.
